title: sedum album subsp. gypsicola
intro: Sedum album subsp. gypsicola, also known as Gypsum-leaved White Sedum, is a subspecies of the Sedum album plant, which is a succulent perennial native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, which is attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Sedum album subsp. gypsicola include its potential to protect against oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and ointments for topical application and internal consumption.
